title: Close the third hermeticity hole: BEE_AGENT_NAME leaks into every spawned suite and manufactures a false red
action: `scripts/run_verify.mjs:392-397` `childEnv()` scrubs CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and BEE_SESSION_ID from every spawned suite (hardening-1-7-10 D1) — and misses BEE_AGENT_NAME, which is the THIRD identity variable and the one bee's OWN swarm rule mandates on write-heavy commands (AGENTS.md critical rule 5). The two rules collide: a worker that obeys rule 5 and then runs the configured verify gets a FALSE RED. Reproduced exactly: `env -u BEE_AGENT_NAME node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs` -> 31 passed 0 failed; `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs` -> 28 passed 3 failed, all three being 'the acting session's own hold must never block its own write' — because the leaked name becomes the acting agent identity inside checkWrite. This cost okf-integration-close-f4 twice: two workers reported it as flaky, and the orchestrator misdiagnosed it as concurrent-chain-run noise. FIX: add `delete env.BEE_AGENT_NAME;` to childEnv() and extend its comment to name all three variables and this exact collision (rule 5 vs the verify command). DEFENSE IN DEPTH, exactly as hardening-1-7-10 did for the other two: delete BEE_AGENT_NAME at bootstrap in `skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs` too, so the suite is hermetic even when run directly rather than through the chain. RED-FIRST: capture the two reproduction outputs above BEFORE the fix, and after the fix show that `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs` is GREEN — that is the real acceptance test, since the whole point is that the chain survives the prefix bee itself mandates. Do NOT weaken any guard assertion — the guard is correct; the harness was leaking.

truths: red evidence: both reproduction commands quoted, showing 31/0 without the var and 28/3 with it
green evidence: BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 on all 79 suites — the chain now survives the prefix bee's own critical rule 5 mandates
env -u BEE_AGENT_NAME node scripts/run_verify.mjs also exits 0 (no regression for the clean path)
test_guards.mjs is hermetic when run DIRECTLY with the var set, not only through the chain
prohibitions: never weaken or delete a write-guard assertion — the guard is correct, the harness was leaking
do not scrub anything beyond the three identity variables
do not regenerate derived artifacts unless a templates/lib or skill file is actually touched; if one is, run all four (mirror, render, manifest, self-onboard)

trace.outcome: Closed the third hermeticity hole: BEE_AGENT_NAME leaked from the calling harness into every suite the verify chain spawns, became the acting agent identity inside the write guard, and turned 'the acting session's own hold must never block its own write' into a false red. Two of bee's own rules collided — critical rule 5 mandates the prefix on write-heavy commands, the preamble mandates running verify, and obeying both manufactured a failure that reads exactly like a real coordination defect. It fooled two workers and the orchestrator in one sitting. run_verify's env scrub already covered two identity variables for precisely this reason and was one short; it now covers all three, with the collision named in the comment, plus a bootstrap scrub in the suite for standalone runs. Acceptance is that the chain now survives the prefix bee itself mandates: 79/79 green with BEE_AGENT_NAME set, and 79/79 green without it. No guard assertion was touched.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE, reproduced deterministically on the unmodified tree — the SAME suite, the SAME command, differing only by one environment variable: `env -u BEE_AGENT_NAME node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs` -> 31 passed, 0 failed. `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs` -> 28 passed, 3 failed. The three failures verbatim: (1) 'checkWrite: a cross-session hold denies another session's write in swarming-with-execution-approved (phase-independence, C8)' failing on 'the acting session's own hold must never block its own write, got {allow:false,kind:reservation,...}'; (2) 'checkWrite: with NO sessionId, a session-owned hold on the target path is never even consulted' failing on 'no sessionId means the hold check never runs — the write-guard behaves exactly as it did before fsh-7'; (3) 'NET branch 4 — cross-session hold: deny shape' failing on 'own hold never blocks'. The guard was correct in all three; the HARNESS was leaking its own identity into the child.",
  "why_it_matters": "This is a collision between two of bee's own rules. AGENTS.md critical rule 5 mandates prefixing write-heavy shell commands with BEE_AGENT_NAME=<name> during swarms; the session preamble mandates running the configured verify. Obeying both produces a FALSE RED, and a false red in the write-guard suites is the worst possible place for one — it reads exactly like a real coordination defect. It cost this feature twice in one sitting: two execution workers reported it as flake, and the orchestrator misdiagnosed it as concurrent-chain-run noise and told the user so before the third worker found the real cause.",
  "root_cause": "run_verify's childEnv() (scripts/run_verify.mjs:392) already scrubbed CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and BEE_SESSION_ID for exactly this hermeticity reason (hardening-1-7-10 D1) and simply missed the third identity variable. Same defect class as the fix that created it, one variable short.",
  "green_evidence": "AFTER: `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs` -> PASS 79 suite(s), exit 0 — this is the real acceptance test, because the whole point is that the chain survives the prefix bee itself mandates. `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` (clean path, no regression) -> PASS 79 suite(s), exit 0. `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs` -> 31 passed, 0 failed, so the suite is now hermetic run DIRECTLY, not only through the chain.",
  "fix_shape": "One `delete env.BEE_AGENT_NAME;` beside the two that already existed, plus a comment block naming all three variables and the rule-5-vs-verify collision explicitly so the next reader does not have to rediscover it. Defense in depth: a bootstrap delete in test_guards.mjs so the suite is hermetic standalone.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Diff read in full. NO guard assertion was weakened, loosened, or deleted — verified by reading the diff, which touches only the env scrub and a comment. One inaccuracy in the worker's comment was corrected by the orchestrator before accept: it described the bootstrap delete as sitting 'alongside the two already deleted below', but the later occurrences in this file are a DIFFERENT mechanism — per-test save/delete/restore pairs that set the variable deliberately to exercise the swarming branch. The comment now says so, and names what the bootstrap delete actually gives them: a clean value to restore to. Derived artifacts re-rendered and the manifest re-written by the orchestrator because test_guards.mjs sits under the rendered skill surface; the .bee/bin mirror and the managed-hash ledger were untouched and confirmed green, since this is neither a templates/lib nor a .bee/bin file.",
  "chain": "Both invocations re-run INDEPENDENTLY by the orchestrator after the comment correction and after the render + manifest write, not accepted on the worker's word: with the prefix and without, 79/79, exit 0 both times."
}
